reviews.review_text:
Chained Together is the kind of game that tests both your platforming skills and your friendships at the same time. Every climb feels intense because one wrong jump can pull the entire team down, which creates some of the funniest and most chaotic moments I’ve had in co-op gaming. The controls are simple, but the teamwork and timing needed to succeed make every section feel rewarding.

It can definitely be frustrating at times, especially after losing progress from a tiny mistake, but that’s also what makes finally reaching a checkpoint feel so satisfying. If you enjoy chaotic multiplayer games that create unforgettable moments with friends, this game is absolutely worth playing.
A very interesting indie game. However this game does require a little bit techniques so if you are new gamer or you are not confident about your gaming skills it's not a good choice for you

With enough patience and medicinal use, I was able too bare chains with two mental ill friends who turned this game into a bare bone miserable experience I ever had to play. 
-Note Friends will have broken your trust, leave you for dust and most all. They will make you do it all again.

congrats on myself for surviving chained together with a cs2 player because that deserves a medal honestly, but please consider this a public service announcement to never play this game unless you want to be gaslit into believing a physics engine failure is 100% your fault. i spent the entire climb getting screamed at about crosshair placement and throwing the match every time we fell, as if we were rushing b in matchmaking and not literally tied by a metal rope trying to jump over a floating pillar, so if you value your sanity and don't want to hear tactical callouts in literal hell, just stay far away because it turns out cs2 brainrot is highly contagious and fatal to healthy communication with your peers and elite circle of companions

I played this game with my 3 lowest IQ friends. When we finished the game, there were only 2 lowest IQ friends left. Good game.

On a more serious note, the game is fun although challenging. I feel like most of the challenge comes from coordinating and communicating with your friends though, you get used to the jumps easily and they get easy after that.
I also feel like the game has more complex jumps at the beginning (first third), mixing several traps together, while the rest of the game is pretty straightforward until the very end where it gets a little bit more complicated.
This game took every last ounce of my patience I was pushed to my absolute limits on the verge of a brain spasm and aneurism at the same time.
Its fun and I had a bunch of yelling moment with my friends.
Great game, i had so much fun with my friend playing it. We had some connection issues but it only made it funnier. Love this game and i hope for sequel or DLC, whatever, PLS GIVE ME MORE!!

Chained Together is one of those games that can either create peak comedy or absolute rage depending on what happens in the next 5 seconds. I first played it solo and later with a group of friends, and honestly the multiplayer experience is where the game shines the most. Trying to coordinate jumps while everyone is chained together creates some genuinely hilarious moments, especially when one person messes up and sends the entire squad flying off the map.

The movement system feels good and the chain mechanic itself is pretty unique, but the level design started to feel repetitive after a while. At first the stages feel creative, but later it becomes more like the same ideas wearing different clothes. If you've played a lot of Roblox obby games, the gameplay will feel very familiar and probably easier than intended.

The difficulty itself is actually balanced well enough, but the optimisation seriously hurts the experience. Constant frame drops and stutters — even on a beefy graphics card — make some sections unnecessarily frustrating. The gameplay can feel janky at times because of this, which makes it hard for me to fully recommend the game.

That said, if you have a group of friends and just want a chaotic game to laugh, scream, and coordinate together in, there's definitely fun to be had here. It's one of those games that's carried more by the moments you create with friends than the actual gameplay itself.

I only played the game solo. And it was a pleasant experience.

It's not that difficult. But it's challenging enough to make the game interesting.

While the game as a whole isn't groundbreaking, everything is well-designed to prevent frustration and encourage you to keep playing.

And for small games like this, you expect to have fun.

Mission accomplished.

Fall and get up / 10

Chained Together was so much fun to play with my friend Gaia. It is one of those games that is hilarious, stressful, and painful all at the same time. ⋆˚꩜｡

The gameplay is simple but chaotic. You are chained to the people you are playing with, and you have to climb higher and higher while trying not to drag each other down. Every jump matters, every mistake hurts, and teamwork is very much required... even when everyone is panicking. ✧

I loved this game, but oh my gosh, we kept falling and starting over. We did not realize you could restart from checkpoints, so we spent about **2 hours** falling and going all the way back to the beginning.

Bro. ˚₊‧꒰ა ☆ ໒꒱ ‧₊˚

Even though that was painful, it honestly made the experience even funnier. There were so many moments of panic, screaming, blaming, laughing, and trying again because we were too stubborn to stop. ⟡

Overall, Chained Together is a really fun co-op game if you enjoy teamwork, chaos, climbing, and suffering with your friends. Just please learn how checkpoints work before you accidentally torture yourself for hours. ✨
i would recommend this game to others because it is fun to play with friends

Chained Together is pure co-op chaos in the best possible way.

The concept is simple, but the second someone misses a jump, the entire group immediately suffers for it. What starts as careful teamwork usually turns into panic, screaming, laughing, and blaming each other within minutes.

Despite how frustrating it can be sometimes, that’s exactly what makes the game so fun. Every bit of progress feels earned, and every failure becomes a memorable moment.

Definitely one of those games that’s way better with friends and voice chat.

The game looks really great, and I bought it when it came on sale (even though it costs about the same as two meals in my country), but unfortunately I couldn't play the game with my friends. Every time we entered together and progressed a little, the game would kick us out or kick one of us . We tried for half an hour of playing and couldn't, so I got a refund.

Chained Together is one of the most chaotic, fun, and genuinely unique co-op games I’ve played in a long time. The concept is simple but brilliant—you and your teammates are literally chained together, trying to climb your way up through difficult obstacles. Sounds easy? It’s not. And that’s exactly what makes it so addictive. (Best played with friends for maximum chaos, laughs, and unforgettable moments.DDD)

A good challenge you can put yourself into. Give it a try if you want to ragequit eventally. Like the game, though, it's not just climbing and jumping: it still has a bunch of mechanics and tricks in store. A decent project.
This is the greatest game i have ever played, I actually shed tears when i completed the game, To the developers of the game, PLEASE RELEASE A SEQUEL!

At the start, this game was absolutely ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ up my mind. I was about to blow up my PC sometimes. Especially when I tried playing with my friend — if the sync messes up, the game gets so frustrating that your brain just goes blank. For the first 4–5 hours, my mind was completely ♥♥♥♥♥♥ up.

But after me and my friend finally got things working, we completed the whole game in duo mode in around 6 hours straight. Then I even tried the 2-hour challenge mission, which was actually fun and challenging, and I managed to get the 2-hour achievement too.

I played this game for around 12 hours total, and honestly, it was fun. If you’re someone who hates games that mentally torture you like  or , then maybe don’t touch this game. But in my opinion, this game is still way more tolerable than those games. Those games completely destroy your sanity. This one is frustrating, but not THAT horrifying, so even a normal player can finish it.

And yeah, even I completed it and got the 2-hour achievement too.

Solid 7.5/10. Good game overall.
This game was a lot of fun and can be challenging depending on the type of play. I'd recommend to others.

[h1]CHAINED TOGETHER: FRIENDSHIP TEST SIMULATOR[/h1]

[hr][/hr]
[h2]OVERVIEW:[/h2]

Chained Together is pure chaos in the best possible way. The entire game is built around one simple idea : you and your friends are chained together while trying to climb impossible obstacles. What starts as teamwork quickly turns into betrayal, screaming and laughter loud enough to wake the neighborhood.

This game does not just test platforming skill. It tests friendships.

[hr][/hr]
[h3]GRAPHICS — ★★★★☆[/h3]
Simple visuals, but the environments do their job well. The focus is entirely on movement and the chaos between players.

[h3]GAMEPLAY — ★★★★★[/h3]
Easy to understand, difficult to master. One bad jump can destroy twenty minutes of progress instantly.

[h3]MULTIPLAYER — ★★★★★[/h3]
This is where the game becomes legendary. Every friend group creates its own chaos.

[h3]REPLAYABILITY — ★★★★☆[/h3]
Even after clearing sections, you keep coming back because every run becomes a completely different disaster.

[h3]SOUND DESIGN — ★★★★☆[/h3]
Most of the memorable audio comes from your friends yelling into the microphone after falling.

[hr][/hr]
[h2]THE BREAKDOWN:[/h2]

[h3]THE TRUE ENEMY IS YOUR FRIENDS:[/h3]

The funniest moments are never planned. Someone misses a jump, panics and suddenly drags the entire squad into the abyss. Half the game is climbing. The other half is blaming each other.

I discovered very quickly that the chain can be weaponized.

There is a special kind of evil joy in waiting until your friend is mid-jump before pulling the chain just enough to ruin their landing while you barely survive the jump yourself. Hearing everyone lose their minds afterward somehow makes failing fun.

[h3]THE CHAOS OF TEAMWORK:[/h3]

Progress only happens when everyone syncs together. One person rushing ahead can ruin everything. One person hesitating can also ruin everything. Communication slowly turns from normal callouts into desperate screaming and accusations within minutes.

And somehow, that chaos is exactly what makes the game so good.

[h3]THE PAIN OF FALLING:[/h3]

Few games recreate the emotional damage of losing massive progress this perfectly. Watching the group slowly realize that everyone is about to fall together creates immediate panic. The silence after a huge mistake is honestly hilarious.

[h3]THE BEST KIND OF MULTIPLAYER GAME:[/h3]

Chained Together feels less like a normal game and more like a shared memory generator. Every session creates moments your friend group will bring up weeks later. The accidental betrayals, impossible saves and dumb mistakes become the actual experience.

[hr][/hr]
[h2]FINAL THOUGHTS:[/h2]

Chained Together is stressful, hilarious and dangerously good at exposing who your real enemies are inside the friend group.

You will fall constantly.
You will blame each other constantly.
And you will absolutely queue up for another run immediately afterward.
Mid range PC and lagging the whole time with friends. Not a good time unless you like being the butt end of a joke.

I think Chained Together is good value if you have a full party to play it with. Don't be ashamed of using some of the checkpoint mechanics if you get too frustrated; plenty of sections are surprisingly difficult, even without the chain.

I bought this thinking there would be random matchmaking.

There is not.

That single realization basically destroyed the entire purchase for me because the whole appeal was imagined chaos with strangers. Without that, the experience becomes heavily dependent on already having a group willing to engage in coordinated suffering.

And if you don’t?

You mostly just stare at the install icon wondering why you bought it.

Not recommended.
